mechanism_narrative: Higher federal funds rates compress equity valuations through increased discount rates applied to future cash flows, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns are positive in greater than 65 percent of periods when FEDFUNDS increases, or the signal Rank IC remains below 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 4.0 percent, labor market slack triggers corporate earnings revisions downward, causing immediate negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in greater than 60 percent of instances where UNRATE crosses above 4.0 percent, or the signal loses statistical significance in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts signals synchronized consumer demand recovery, driving positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict positive 60-day returns in greater than 55 percent of cases, or the interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production below the 2017 baseline indicates supply-side contraction that outweighs demand resilience, producing negative equity returns during tightening cycles.
failure_condition: SPX generates positive 20-day returns in greater than 60 percent of periods when INDPRO falls below 100 during tightening, or predictive power vanishes outside the specified regime.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price index levels above 295 trigger aggressive central bank policy expectations, compressing equity multiples and generating negative 5-day forward returns.
failure_condition: 5-day returns are positive in greater than 65 percent of observations when CPIAUCSL exceeds 295, or the signal shows negative Rank IC over rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total employment combined with falling unemployment rates confirms labor market tightening that supports corporate revenue growth, yielding positive 20-day S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive returns in greater than 58 percent of cases, or the interaction effect is statistically indistinguishable from zero in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly GDP growth above trend signals sustained economic expansion that lifts corporate earnings expectations, producing positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are negative in greater than 60 percent of periods following positive GDPC1 revisions, or the signal Rank IC remains below 0.01 across all test windows.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production exceeding 104 reflects robust manufacturing capacity utilization that drives immediate equity price appreciation during early expansion phases.
failure_condition: 5-day returns are negative in greater than 62 percent of instances when INDPRO surpasses 104 in early cycles, or the threshold effect disappears in out-of-sample validation.
